- The white region at cols 6-13 changed from rows 54-57 to rows 52-57 (expanded upward by 2 rows)
- The charcoal at cols 8-10 moved from rows 51-52 to rows 49-50 (moved up by 2 rows)

- The white region at cols 18-23 disappeared (was rows 56-57, now gone)
So clicking the blue block at cols 12-13 moved the objects in the track at cols 8-10 upward by 2 rows. The white region expanded to fill the space.
This makes sense - the blue blocks control vertical movement of the objects in adjacent tracks.
Let me understand the structure better:
- Track at cols 8-10: charcoal + green + white, controlled by blue at cols 12-13
- Track at cols 14-15: green + black, controlled by blue at cols 16-17

- Track at cols 41-43: charcoal + purple + white, controlled by blue at cols 38-39 or 46-47
- Track at cols 48-49: yellow + black, controlled by blue at cols 46-47 or 50-51
- Track at cols 52-54: charcoal + yellow + black + white, controlled by blue at cols 50-51
The blue blocks seem to be paired with adjacent tracks. Let me figure out which blue block controls which track.

- Blue at cols 46-47 → maybe controls track at cols 48-49

Actually, looking more carefully, the blue blocks are positioned between tracks or next to them. Let me think about this differently.
The blue block at cols 12-13 is to the right of the white region at cols 6-13. When clicked, it moved the objects in the cols 8-10 track upward.
